summaryText: Guest speaker Montell Jordan, returning to Impact Church, delivered a message titled 'Faith for the Future.' He opened by expressing his honor to be back and his deep friendship with Pastors Trav and Natalie. The core message centered on the transformative power of being in Christ, emphasizing that in Him, we become new creations and our past does not define our destiny. Pastor Montell shared five key steps to increase faith for the future, each warning against allowing external or internal factors to limit God's plans. He used personal stories, including his transition from a music career to ministry and recent family losses, to illustrate how God redeems our stories. He powerfully challenged the congregation not to be defined by past mistakes, personal pain, other people's opinions, financial status, or earthly titles, but to find their identity in Christ alone. The sermon concluded with a heartfelt altar call, emphasizing that salvation and the assurance of eternity are the ultimate foundation for our future hope. The tone was encouraging, transparent, and focused on spiritual growth and obedience.
mainPoints: Do not allow your past or your mistakes to define your future; in Christ, you are a new creation.
Do not allow your pain, trauma, or other people's opinions to define your future; God's purpose prevails.
Do not allow your financial status (paper) or your earthly position/title to define your future; your identity in Christ defines what you do.

versesMentioned.text: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come: The old has gone, the new is here!
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.
The Lord is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
‘He will wipe every tear from their eyes. There will be no more death’ or mourning or crying or pain, for the old order of things has passed away.”
Many are the plans in a person’s heart, but it is the Lord’s purpose that prevails.
For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
Let everyone be subject to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except that which God has established. The authorities that exist have been established by God.

Monday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 5:17 (NIV) - 'Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, the new creation has come...'
Monday.action: Write down one thing from your past that you have allowed to define you. Pray and consciously release its hold, declaring you are a new creation.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that in you I am made new. Help me to see myself through your eyes of grace today.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 147:3 (NIV) - 'He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.'
Tuesday.action: Identify an area of past hurt or trauma. Spend time in quiet reflection, inviting God to bring His healing to that specific memory.
Tuesday.prayer: Healer, I bring my pain to you. Please bind up my wounds and help me not to be defined by what was done to me.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 19:21 (NIV) - '...it is the Lord’s purpose that prevails.'
Wednesday.action: Recall a recent criticism or negative opinion you received. Counter it by writing down one truth from God's Word about who He says you are.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, help me to value your purpose for my life more than the plans or opinions of others. Anchor my heart in your truth.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 6:21 (NIV) - 'For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.'
Thursday.action: Review your giving. If you tithe, thank God for the opportunity for obedience. If not, prayerfully take a step of obedience in this area this week.
Thursday.prayer: God, I want my heart to be where yours is. Teach me the joy of obedience in my finances, trusting you as my provider.

Friday.scripture: Romans 13:1 (NIV) - '...there is no authority except that which God has established.'
Friday.action: Reflect on your primary roles (e.g., parent, employee, volunteer). Thank God for the calling behind each title, not just the title itself.
Friday.prayer: Lord, help me to wear my earthly titles loosely. Let my identity in you define how I serve in every position you've placed me.

Saturday.scripture: Jeremiah 29:11 (NIV) - 'For I know the plans I have for you...plans to give you hope and a future.'
Saturday.action: Write down one hope or dream for your future. Present it to God in prayer, asking Him to increase your faith for it according to His will.
Saturday.prayer: God of all hope, I trust you with my future. Increase my faith to believe you have good plans for me, both now and for eternity.
